what_it_does: Provides synchronous, file-based exclusive locks by wrapping the proper-lockfile package, recording the holder's PID in a diagnostic file, and exposing acquire/release/read helpers with a clear contention-vs-I/O-error policy.
observations: Wraps proper-lockfile's atomic mkdir + mtime-heartbeat protocol.
Uses a 10-minute stale threshold (DEFAULT_STALE_MS) for crashed-holder recovery.
Creates the lock target file with fs.openSync('a') before locking so proper-lockfile has an existing path.
Returns null on ELOCKED contention and throws other I/O errors.
Records process.pid into the lockPath file as a best-effort diagnostic.
releaseFileLock calls the underlying release and then unlinks the diagnostic file, swallowing errors.
readLockHolder parses the diagnostic PID file but treats the PID as informational only.
standouts: Separates the real mutex directory (<lockPath>.lock/) from the human-readable diagnostic PID file (<lockPath>).
Explicit error contract: null means contention, unexpected I/O errors are thrown.
Relies on proper-lockfile's race-free stale-stealing rather than a naive PID-file scheme.
conventions: Default constant exported as a module-level const.
Best-effort cleanup with empty catch blocks.
JSDoc block comments explaining on-disk layout and error policy.
concerns: releaseFileLock and the post-acquire PID write swallow all errors silently, which can hide disk problems after the initial lock.
The diagnostic PID file is unlinked on release, so readLockHolder can return null even when a lock is momentarily being stolen or recreated.
The try/catch around fs.openSync that only re-throws is redundant.
parseInt on a partial numeric string would still return a number, although Number.isFinite and pid > 0 mitigate some cases.
notes: A defensive, synchronous wrapper around proper-lockfile that adds a diagnostic PID file and a strict null-for-contention, throw-for-I/O error contract.
questions: Is the 10-minute stale threshold ever overridden by callers?
How do embedding migration and sync --background invoke this helper?
What monitoring exists for locks that stay held near the stale threshold?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of providing race-free, machine-local exclusive file locks with diagnostic holder PIDs matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Safe concurrency through proven external primitives.
Clear separation of mutex state and observability data.
Fail-loud on real I/O problems while treating contention as a normal outcome.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give long-running background tasks a reliable 'only one at a time' guard on a single machine, while making it easy to see which process currently holds the lock and to recover automatically from a crashed holder.
novelty: The distinct element is the deliberate two-file design: a proper-lockfile mutex directory for correctness and a separate diagnostic PID file for operator visibility, plus an explicit null-vs-throw contention policy.
contribution: A reusable, synchronous file-lock abstraction that contributes clear acquire/release semantics, automatic stale-lock recovery, a diagnostic holder PID, and safe cleanup for background singleton work.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that wraps proper-lockfile to provide synchronous exclusive file locks with a 10-minute stale threshold, recording the holder's PID in a diagnostic file and returning null on contention while throwing unexpected I/O errors.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to let long-running background operations such as embedding migration and background sync run as a single instance per machine by acquiring a robust machine-local file lock.

symbols.does: Describes a held file lock as a path plus a release callback.
symbols.description: A small interface that pairs the lock file path with a release function, letting callers hold and later release a file lock without needing to know the underlying lockfile implementation details.

symbols.code_id: code://company/recollection/src/file-lock.ts::acquireFileLock | function | export function acquireFileLock(lockPath: string): FileLockHandle | null {
symbols.does: Synchronously acquires an exclusive file lock or returns null if it is already held.
symbols.description: Ensures the parent directory and target file exist, then calls proper-lockfile's lockSync with no retries and a 10-minute stale threshold. On ELOCKED it returns null; other errors propagate. After acquiring, it best-effort writes the current process PID into the lockPath file for diagnostics.

symbols.code_id: code://company/recollection/src/file-lock.ts::releaseFileLock | function | export function releaseFileLock(handle: FileLockHandle): void {
symbols.does: Releases a previously acquired lock and removes its diagnostic PID file.
symbols.description: Calls the underlying lockfile release callback and then unlinks the diagnostic lockPath file, swallowing any errors so release attempts do not crash the caller.

symbols.code_id: code://company/recollection/src/file-lock.ts::readLockHolder | function | export function readLockHolder(lockPath: string): number | null {
symbols.does: Reads and parses the diagnostic PID from a lock file, if present and valid.
symbols.description: A best-effort helper that reads the lockPath file, parses an integer, and returns a positive finite PID or null. The result is informational because the actual mutex lives in the adjacent .lock/ directory.
